_comment: Single source of truth for the causal-repair benchmark. Model ids are OpenRouter ids verified live 2026-07. The ':floor' suffix asks OpenRouter to route to the cheapest provider for that model. Set reps and max_tokens to trade cost vs statistical confidence.
_prices_comment: OpenRouter USD per 1M tokens (verified 2026-07). Used for real cost, since Claude Code's reported total_cost_usd misprices non-Anthropic models routed via the proxy.

C4-qwen-mimo-harness.role: treatment: causal-repair harness on Qwen+MiMo only (v0.3 workflow, measured 2026-07-12)

C5-harness-v2.role: treatment: Ledger-Relay v0.4.0 harness on Qwen+MiMo (contract-completeness gate)

C6-prompt-only.role: ablation (H5): identical v0.4.0 skill text + scripts, but NO hook enforcement — isolates mechanical enforcement as the causal ingredient

C7-pcr.role: treatment v0.4.1 (Proof-Carrying Repair): C5 + per-clause witness gate (contract tests must fail on base and kill mutants). extra_prompt is the only difference from C5, isolating PCR.

C8-pcr-loop.role: treatment v0.4.2: C7 + runner-enforced gates (CI-style outer loop: verify-witnesses + change-surface coverage run by the RUNNER; on failure the model is re-invoked once with the gate output). Diffs from C7: witness_retries + coverage_gate.

C9-qwen-retry.role: confound control: plain Qwen baseline + an equal-budget outer retry loop that re-invokes on VISIBLE-test failure (no plugin, no gate). Isolates 'is the harness gain just retrying?' A gamed patch passes the visible test, so this loop cannot fix gaming.

Q27-baseline.role: 27B replication: plain Qwen3.6-27B baseline (the real intranet model, not the 35B-A3B used elsewhere).

Q27-harness-v2.role: 27B replication: full v0.4.2 harness on Qwen3.6-27B (+MiMo). Does the harness help or HURT on the weaker 27B model? Mirror of C8.

Q27-harness-c5.role: 27B replication of the user's ACTUAL setup: v0.4.0-style harness (contract-clause gate only, NO PCR witnesses, NO coverage gate, NO runner-enforced retry loop). Mirror of C5 on Qwen3.6-27B - this is what their intranet reportedly runs.
